digest.component_or_area: rebar identity entity + authorship certification + Jira reconciler mapping
propositions: rebar's author field is an unauthenticated git config user.name that anyone with push access can forge, so ticket events lack auditable, non-repudiable attribution to a real person.
An identity is modeled as a first-class event-sourced entity storing name, email, {provider,external_id} mappings, and public keys only, with a write-time guard rejecting private-key material.

Authorship signatures are a new attestation kind (rebar.authorship.v1) wrapping an in-toto Statement whose subject binds {event_uuid, content_hash} in a DSSE envelope, verified offline against the identity's in-band public keys, with unsigned Tier-0 fallback that never blocks writes.

Key validity uses TOFU on a self-signed genesis key plus G2 (key changes must be signed by a currently-valid key), with era scoped by tickets-branch commit ancestry refined by intra_idx rather than HLC or wall-clock.

A provider-neutral {provider, external_id} mapping (Jira first adapter) resolves outbound assignee/reporter by accountId with a best-effort bootstrap and graceful fallback chain, and unmapped inbound users mint idempotently auto-upgrading placeholders.

Enforcement is opt-in via identity.require_authenticated and a CI merge-gate verify-identity job that fails on unsigned/unverified events since identity.enforce_since, while default install stays byte-identical and replay never rejects.
